user_message: Just came out of competitive positioning with Blue Yonder Airlines. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 16:31 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with competitive positioning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 14:38 EST
Subject: Re: competitive positioning Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about competitive positioning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-12 13:52 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Blue Yonder Airlines competitive positioning

There's been a change in our timeline for competitive positioning. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Champion-Change Email Signal -> Re-engagement Plan template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
